# Task 02.7: Query Set and Relevance Judgments

## Sprint Info
- **Duration:** 4 days
- **Milestone:** M1.5 - Search Quality Evaluation Baseline
- **Priority:** P0
- **Depends On:** Task 02.6
- **Blocks:** Tasks 03.1, 05.1, 08.1, 09.3

## What You'll Build
Create the first durable offline evaluation set so retrieval, ranking, freshness, and spam changes can be measured before advanced features are added.

## Daily Breakdown

### Day 1: Query Taxonomy
- [ ] Define query buckets: navigational, informational, transactional, local, news/freshness, typo, mixed Persian/English, long-tail.
- [ ] Define Persian/Iran-specific buckets: Persian digits, Arabic digit variants, ZWNJ variants, Iranian brands, local services, government domains, commerce queries.

### Day 2: Query Collection
- [ ] Create an initial seed set of at least 300 queries.
- [ ] Include at least 150 Persian or Iran-local queries.
- [ ] Mark each query with language, script, intent, freshness need, vertical, and difficulty.

### Day 3: Judgment Format
- [ ] Define graded relevance labels: 0 bad, 1 weak, 2 acceptable, 3 excellent.
- [ ] Define duplicate, spam, stale, and unsafe-result flags.
- [ ] Store judgments in a versioned file format.

### Day 4: Review and Baseline Run
- [ ] Run the current M1 retrieval baseline against the query set.
- [ ] Record failure examples for the top 25 worst queries.
- [ ] Add reviewer notes and known limitations.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] At least 300 versioned queries exist.
- [ ] At least 150 queries represent Persian or Iran-local use cases.
- [ ] Each query has intent, language/script, freshness need, and vertical metadata.
- [ ] At least 100 queries have top-10 graded relevance judgments.
- [ ] Baseline failure examples are documented before M2/M3/M6 work starts.

## Required Evidence
- Evaluation dataset location.
- Baseline run ID.
- Metrics snapshot.
- Top failure examples with suggested next task owner.
